title: Pouvoirs britanniques

pages.text:
ARCHIVES

.57 NOV 190%

Whereas, for the better treating

of and arranging

certain matters which are now in discussion,

or waich may come

into discussion,

between the Government of the United Kingdom

of Great Britain

and Northern Ireland and the Governments of

Canada, Australia, New Zealand, the Union of South Africa, India and Pakistan of the one part, and the Goverment of the French

Republio of the other part, relative to British Commonwealth

War Graves in French territory, it is expedient that a fit person should be invested with Full Power to conduct the said discussion on the part of the Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland: I, Erest Bevin, a

Member of His Majesty's Most Honourable Privy Council, 8c., 8c.,

His Majesty's Principal Secretary of State for Foreign Affairs, do hereby certify that sir Oliver Charles Harvey, Knight Grand

Cross of the Most Distinguished Order of Saint Michael and

Saint George, Companion of the Most Honourable Order of the

Bath, His Majesty's Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary at Paris, is by these Presents named, constituted and appointed

as Plenipotentiary and Representative having Full Power and

Authority to agree and conclude with such Plenipotentiaries or

Representatives as may be vested with similar Power and Author ity on the part of the Goverrments as aforesaid any Treaty,

Convention, Agreement or other Instrument that may tend to the attainment of the above-mentioned end, and to sign for the

Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern

Ireland, everything so agreed upon and concluded. Further, I do hereby certify that whatever things shall be so transacted and concluded by the said Plenipotentiary and Representative, shall, subject if necessary to Ratification by the Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, be agreed to, acknowledged and accepted by the said covernment of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland in the fullest manner.

In witness whereof I have signed these Presents and

affixed hereto my Seal.

Signed and sealed at the Foreign Office, London, the

Seventeenth day of March, in the Year of our Lord, One thousand

Nine hundred and Fifty.

Ten erin

FULL POWER to :- SIR OLIVER CHARLES HARVEY, G.G.M.O., to degotiato mitt the dovernment of the

French Republle relatire to British

Commonreaith War Grates in French territory.
